title: APEDA Registration Online

hero.headline: Export Agri Products with APEDA Certification
hero.subtext: Get your APEDA registration with Professionals Law for lifetime export compliance support.

mainHeading: APEDA Registration Online – APEDA Licence Registration Service

introduction.heading: Introduction and Its Compliance
body: APEDA Registration APEDA (Agricultural and Processed Food Products Export Development Authority) Registration Act, 1985 APEDA is required for companies / businesses intending to export schedule products. The registration is one time and is further necessary for exporters for getting the benefit /assistance under the Government schemes from time to time. Orders through such registered exporters take priority for sales, delivery & price preference. Quotations from unregistered exporters are not considered for release of orders. Professionals Law assists its clients with a hassle free APEDA registration by taking care of the documentation, application and ongoing compliance requirements.

whyNeeded.heading: Why It Is Needed
body: APEDA Registration is compulsory for export of scheduled products that covers fruits, vegetables, dairy, poultry, cereals and processed foods etc. It is the necessary target of the export permit and a variety of government subsidy policy support. Businesses are able to guarantee export compliance and easier market access to international markets with Professionals Law.

body.title: Export Promotion Support
body.description: APEDA registration allows exporters to avail financial assistance, participate in international trade fairs and access promotional schemes under the Ministry of Commerce.

body.title: Market Expansion
body.description: It enables registered exporters to expand their business by entering foreign markets with the backing and certification of APEDA, increasing global trust.

body.title: Training and Guidelines
body.description: APEDA provides guidelines, quality standards and training to exporters, helping them meet global product specifications and boost exports.

body.title: Networking Opportunities
body.description: Registered exporters get opportunities to connect with international buyers through APEDA’s buyer-seller meets and digital platforms.

body.title: One-Time Registration
body.description: APEDA registration is a one-time process with lifetime validity, making it a long-term investment for every food/agri exporter.

criteria: Any individual, firm, company or organization involved in the export of scheduled products listed under APEDA is eligible.
The business must have an Import Export Code (IEC) issued by the Director General of Foreign Trade (DGFT).
The applicant must have a valid PAN and bank account in the name of the business.

documents: PAN, Aadhaar card of the business or applicant (KYC documents)
Address proof of the business (electricity bill, lease agreement, etc.)
Memorandum of Association (MOA)/Partnership Deed/Trust Deed (as applicable)
List of products intended to export
Professionals Law ensures that all documents are checked and submitted correctly for smooth approval.

body.description: Gather and verify all mandatory documents.
Register and apply through the APEDA portal.
Pay the prescribed government fee online.
Application and documents are reviewed by APEDA officials.
On successful verification, APEDA Registration Certificate is issued.
Professionals Law handles the entire process efficiently, ensuring that your business gets registered without delays or rejections.

penalties.heading: Penalties
body: Exporters without APEDA registration may be barred from exporting scheduled products.
Failure to follow quality guidelines and standards may result in blacklisting or suspension from the APEDA database.
Misrepresentation of information during registration can lead to legal consequences under the APEDA Act.

body.title: Standard Processing Time
body.description: The APEDA Registration process typically takes 5 to 7 working days from the date of submission of the complete application and payment.

body.question: What is APEDA Registration?
body.answer: APEDA Registration is a mandatory requirement for exporters of scheduled agricultural and food products, governed by the APEDA Act, 1985.

body.question: Is APEDA Registration mandatory for all exporters?
body.answer: It is mandatory only for those exporting products listed under the APEDA schedule.

body.question: How long is APEDA registration valid?
body.answer: APEDA registration is a one-time registration with lifetime validity, subject to regular business compliance.

body.question: Can I apply for APEDA without an Import Export Code (IEC)?
body.answer: No, having an IEC is mandatory to apply for APEDA registration.

body.question: What products are covered under APEDA?
body.answer: Products include fruits, vegetables, meat, poultry, dairy, cereal products, honey, floriculture and processed foods.

body.question: Can I make changes to the certificate after registration?
body.answer: Yes, exporters can update product categories and business information by submitting a modification request on the APEDA portal.

body.question: Does APEDA offer financial assistance?
body.answer: Yes, APEDA provides support for packaging, market development and infrastructure development under its promotional schemes.

body.question: Who can apply for APEDA registration?
body.answer: Any individual, partnership firm, LLP, private/public limited company or cooperative society involved in the export of scheduled products.

body.question: How can Professionals Law help in APEDA Registration?
body.answer: Professionals Law assists in document preparation, application submission, fee payment and compliance, making the process easy and error-free.
